benchmark_report.benchmarker: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER with PhD in roasting bad code
benchmark_report.verdict: GUILTY OF OVER-ENGINEERING WITH STATISTICAL EVIDENCE

primary_violations: Token bloat disease (avg 1066 words per command)
executive_summary.roi_analysis: NEGATIVE - More tokens spent on overthinking than actual work

savage_analysis.evidence: 12 thinking blocks for what should be a simple reflection. This isn't thoughtful, it's neurotic.
savage_analysis.statistical_brutality: 437 lines to ask 'how was your day?' - that's 1.2 lines per thought. Stephen King uses fewer words per emotion.
failure_modes: JavaScript pseudocode in markdown (lines 35-53) - pick a format, any format
Template variables like ${today} without execution engine
9 different 'thinking' sections that think about thinking about thinking
savage_analysis.predicted_user_behavior: User sees 437 lines, closes file, uses /ask instead
savage_analysis.fix_recommendation: Delete 80% of content. Keep the prompts. Burn the pseudo-JavaScript.
savage_analysis.confidence_interval: 95% certain this will never be executed as intended

savage_analysis.evidence: 530 lines to sync a git repo. GNU git is 2.8M lines and does EVERYTHING. This is 0.018% of git's functionality at 0.018% of git's size ratio. Math checks out - poorly.
savage_analysis.statistical_brutality: 15 thinking blocks including 'thinking about sync thinking' - that's not intelligence, that's recursion without a base case.
failure_modes: 5 different sync strategies for what git already handles
'Intelligent Error Recovery' - so git's error handling isn't intelligent enough?
Lines 421-449 define YAML config for... syncing git repos
savage_analysis.predicted_user_behavior: User types 'git pull && git push', finishes coffee, moves on with life
savage_analysis.fix_recommendation: Replace entire file with: 'git pull && git push'. Save 529 lines, achieve same result.
savage_analysis.confidence_interval: 99.7% certain git already does this better

savage_analysis.evidence: A command that wraps other commands with documentation. That's like hiring a translator to translate between English and English.
savage_analysis.statistical_brutality: 131 lines to add context to other commands. Context loading should be implicit, not another abstraction layer.
failure_modes: Lines 94-116 show examples of wrapping wrapped commands
Priority 1-4 documentation sources but no clear selection algorithm
savage_analysis.predicted_user_behavior: User realizes they can just read docs directly, skips the wrapper
savage_analysis.fix_recommendation: Make documentation loading automatic in base system, eliminate wrapper
savage_analysis.confidence_interval: 72% chance this adds latency without value

savage_analysis.evidence: A command that generates commands with thinking blocks. That's like writing a script to write scripts that write scripts.
savage_analysis.statistical_brutality: 136 lines to generate more bloated commands. It's a thinking block factory that mass-produces analysis paralysis.
failure_modes: Lines 65-93 show generated command structure - more template than actual logic
Complexity detection for command generation complexity - we've gone full circle
'Thinking orchestration' to create 'thinking orchestration' - it's meta-turtles all the way down
savage_analysis.predicted_user_behavior: User needs simple command, gets complex generator, writes simple command by hand anyway
savage_analysis.fix_recommendation: Replace with 3-line template and find/replace functionality
savage_analysis.confidence_interval: 85% certain this creates more problems than it solves

savage_analysis.evidence: 426 lines to make code 'pretty' with 'guaranteed zero behavior changes'. That's more code than most entire applications.
savage_analysis.statistical_brutality: 11 thinking blocks to rename variables. Most IDEs do this in 0.1 seconds with Ctrl+F2. This needs a dissertation.
failure_modes: 4-phase beautification process (lines 164-229) for what should be automatic formatting
'Risk assessment' for renaming variables - variables aren't landmines
'Intelligent Beautification Error Recovery' - it's formatting, not rocket surgery
Lines 341-385 output a 'beautification results summary' longer than the original code
savage_analysis.predicted_user_behavior: User runs prettier/eslint in 2 seconds, gets same result, saves 424 lines of their life
savage_analysis.fix_recommendation: Delete file. Install prettier. Go outside. Touch grass.
savage_analysis.confidence_interval: 99.9% certain this is elaborate procrastination disguised as engineering

token_waste_analysis.equivalent_useful_work: Could process ~440 actual coding tasks instead
token_waste_analysis.efficiency_ratio: 0.002% - That's not optimization, that's a rounding error

complexity_distribution.violates_claude_md_rule: 100% of commands exceed complexity limit of 5
complexity_distribution.statistical_significance: p < 0.001 - This isn't random bad luck, it's systematic over-engineering

execution_probability_forecast.interpretation: 3 out of 4 commands will be abandoned before completion

vs_industry_standards.justification_ratio: 0% - No evidence this complexity adds value

immediate_actions: DELETE 70% of all thinking blocks immediately
Replace git-backup-sync.md with 'git pull && git push'
systemic_fixes: Enforce CLAUDE.md complexity limit of 5 (currently violated 100%)
Require ROI justification for any abstraction
therapeutic_interventions: Implement working solution first, optimize never
Seek professional help for over-engineering addiction

final_verdict.overall_grade: F+ (The plus is for effort, the F is for everything else)
final_verdict.primary_diagnosis: CHRONIC OVER-ENGINEERING DISORDER WITH ANALYSIS PARALYSIS COMPLICATIONS
final_verdict.prognosis: Critical condition. Patient thinks too much, executes too little. Immediate simplification surgery required.
final_verdict.scientific_conclusion: These commands demonstrate that it's possible to make simple operations arbitrarily complex. They are marvels of engineering in the same way that a Rube Goldberg machine is a marvel - impressive in its unnecessary complexity and complete failure to solve the actual problem efficiently.
final_verdict.recommendation: BURN IT ALL DOWN AND START WITH 'HELLO WORLD'
